William Davis, born 1617, in Wales. He died 9 Dec 1683 (age 66), at Roxbury, Suffolk, Mass. He married 1st, Elizabeth ..., who died 4 May 1658; married 2nd, 21 …

zh.lifeSummary: 當Elizabeth Chamberlain於1621年出生在英國諾福爾可辛加姆時，她的父親Henry Chamberlin Sr26歲，母親Jane Grace Freeman26歲。她於大約1639年與William Davis在布利提申 可隆尼亞爾 亞美利加馬薩瑟次 貝 可隆尼素福爾可洛波利結婚。他們至少是9個兒子和3個女兒的父母。她去世於1658年5月4日在布利提申 可隆尼亞爾 亞美利加馬薩瑟次 貝 可隆尼素福爾可洛波利享年37歲，埋葬在布利提申 可隆尼亞爾 亞美利加馬薩瑟次 貝 可隆尼素福爾可洛波利。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Elizabeth Chamberlain于1621年出生在英国諾福爾可辛加姆时，她的父亲Henry Chamberlin Sr26岁，母亲Jane Grace Freeman26岁。她于大约1639年与William Davis在布利提申 可隆尼亞爾 亞美利加馬薩瑟次 貝 可隆尼素福爾可洛波利结婚。他们至少是9个儿子和3个女儿的父母。她去世于1658年5月4日在布利提申 可隆尼亞爾 亞美利加馬薩瑟次 貝 可隆尼素福爾可洛波利享年37岁，埋葬在布利提申 可隆尼亞爾 亞美利加馬薩瑟次 貝 可隆尼素福爾可洛波利。

nameMeanings.name: Chamberlain
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: status name from Old French chambrelain, Norman French cambrelanc, cambrelen(c) ‘chamberlain’ (of ancient Germanic origin, from kamer ‘chamber, room’, Latin camera (see Chambers ) + the diminutive suffix -(l)ing). This was originally the name of an official in charge of the private chambers of his master, but is so widespread in late medieval England that it must sometimes have been used of people of more ordinary status, perhaps as a nickname for an officious or self-important person or for someone who played the role of chamberlain in a folk play, tableau, or ceremony. Compare Chancellor for a possible similar usage.

variants.name: Chamberlin
meanings: French: occupational name for an official in charge of the private chambers of his master, from Old French chamberlenc ‘chamberlain’. See also Chamberlain and compare above.

variants.name: Chambers
meanings: from Middle English chaumbre ‘room (in a house); reception room in a palace or official building’ (Old French chambre). It is identical in implied function with the Chamberlain , which denoted an official: to pay in cameram was to pay into the exchequer, of which the camerarius or chamberer was in charge. The surname also applied to clerks employed there. As the office of Chamberlain rose in the social scale, this term remained reserved for more humble servants of the bedchamber or private quarters.
(of Norman origin): habitational name from Les Chambres, Manche (France).

nameMeanings.name: Elizabeth
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: The usual spelling of   Elisabeth   in English. It is recorded in the medieval period, but was made popular by being borne by  Queen Elizabeth I  of England (  1533–1603 ). In the 20th century it again became extremely fashionable, partly because it was the name of  Elizabeth Bowes-Lyon  (  1900–2002 ), who in  1936  became Queen Elizabeth as the wife of King George VI, and after his death in  1952  achieved great public affection as Queen Mother for nearly half a century. Even more influentially, it is the name of her daughter  Queen Elizabeth II  (  b. 1926 ).

variants.name: Elisabeth
meanings: The spelling of Elizabeth used in the Authorized Version of the New Testament, and in most modern European languages. This was the name of the mother of John the Baptist (Luke 1:60). Etymologically, the name means ‘God is my oath’, and is therefore identical with Elisheba, the name of the wife of Aaron according to the genealogy at Exodus 6:23. The final element seems to have been altered by association with Hebrew shabbāth ‘Sabbath’.

historicEvents.title: The English Civil War
historicEvents.description: A series of conflicts regarding England's governance during the years 1642 to 1651 is now known as The English Civil War. Charles I summoned supporters to join him against his enemies in Parliament. In October 1642, nearly 10,000 men fought for Charles I and chased Parliament across the River Tamar. Fighting continued for years and was finally ended at the Battle of Worcester on September 3, 1651, with a Parliamentarian victory.

en.lifeSummary: When Elizabeth Chamberlain was born in 1621, in Hingham, Norfolk, England, her father, Henry Chamberlin Sr, was 26 and her mother, Jane Grace Freeman, was 26. She married William Davis about 1639, in Roxbury, Suffolk, Massachusetts Bay Colony, British Colonial America. They were the parents of at least 9 sons and 3 daughters. She died on 4 May 1658, in Roxbury, Suffolk, Massachusetts Bay Colony, British Colonial America, at the age of 37, and was buried in Roxbury, Suffolk, Massachusetts Bay Colony, British Colonial America.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Elizabeth Chamberlain est née en 1621, à Hingham, Norfolk, Angleterre, son père, Henry Chamberlin Sr, avait 26 ans et sa mère, Jane Grace Freeman, avait 26 ans. Elle a épousé William Davis environ 1639, à Roxbury, Suffolk, Massachusetts Bay Colony, Amérique coloniale britannique. Ils ont eu au moins 9 fils et 3 filles. Elle est décédée le 4 mai 1658, à Roxbury, Suffolk, Massachusetts Bay Colony, Amérique coloniale britannique, à l’âge de 37 ans, et a été enterrée à Roxbury, Suffolk, Massachusetts Bay Colony, Amérique coloniale britannique.

de.lifeSummary: Als Elizabeth Chamberlain 1621 in Hingham, Norfolk, England geboren wurde, war ihr Vater Henry Chamberlin Sr 26 und ihre Mutter Jane Grace Freeman 26. Sie hat um 1639 in Roxbury, Suffolk, Massachusetts Bay Colony, British Colonial America William Davis geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 9 Söhne und 3 Töchter. Sie starb am 4. Mai 1658 im Alter von 37 Jahren in Roxbury, Suffolk, Massachusetts Bay Colony, British Colonial America und wurde in Roxbury, Suffolk, Massachusetts Bay Colony, British Colonial America begraben.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Elizabeth Chamberlain è nata il nel 1621 nel Hingham, Norfolk, England, sua padre, Henry Chamberlin Sr, aveva 26 anni e sua madre, Jane Grace Freeman, aveva 26 anni. Lei sposato William Davis circa 1639, nel Roxbury, Suffolk, Massachusetts Bay Colony, British Colonial America. Erano genitori di almeno 9 figli e 3 figlie. Morì il 4 maggio 1658, nel Roxbury, Suffolk, Massachusetts Bay Colony, British Colonial America, all'età di 37 anni, e fu sepolta a Roxbury, Suffolk, Massachusetts Bay Colony, British Colonial America.

ja.lifeSummary: Elizabeth Chamberlainが1621年にイギリスノーフォルクヒンガムで生まれた時，父Henry Chamberlin Srは26歳，母Jane Grace Freemanは26歳であった。約1639年にイギリス植民地アメリカマッサチウセッツ バイ コロニースフォルクロクスブリーでWilliam Davisと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも9人の息子と3人の娘の親であった。彼女は1658年5月4日にイギリス植民地アメリカマッサチウセッツ バイ コロニースフォルクロクスブリーで37歳で死去し，イギリス植民地アメリカマッサチウセッツ バイ コロニースフォルクロクスブリーにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: Elizabeth Chamberlain(이)가 1621년 영국 노르폴크 잉함에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 Henry Chamberlin Sr(은)는 26세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Jane Grace Freeman(은)는 26세였다. 그녀는 대략 1639년 브리티시 콜론이앨 어머릭아 마작세츠 베이 콜론이 수폴크 록스버이에서 William Davis(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 아홉 명의 아들과 세 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1658년 5월 4일 브리티시 콜론이앨 어머릭아 마작세츠 베이 콜론이 수폴크 록스버이에서 37세의 나이로 사망했으며 브리티시 콜론이앨 어머릭아 마작세츠 베이 콜론이 수폴크 록스버이에 묻혔다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Elizabeth Chamberlain nasceu em 1621, em Hingham, Norfolk, Inglaterra, seu pai, Henry Chamberlin Sr, tinha 26 anos e sua mãe, Jane Grace Freeman, tinha 26 anos. Ela casou-se com William Davis aproximadamente 1639, em Roxbury, Suffolk, Massachusetts Bay Colony, British Colonial America. Eles tiveram pelo menos 9 filhos e 3 filhas. Ela faleceu em 4 de maio de 1658, em Roxbury, Suffolk, Massachusetts Bay Colony, British Colonial America, com 37 anos, e foi sepultada em Roxbury, Suffolk, Massachusetts Bay Colony, British Colonial America.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Elizabeth Chamberlain родилась в 1621 г. в местности хингхам, норфолк, Англия, её отцу по имени Henry Chamberlin Sr было 26 лет, а матери, которую звали Jane Grace Freeman, 26. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени William Davis около 1639 г. в местности роксбури, суффолк, массачусетц беи колони, бритиш колоняль амирика. У них было по крайней мере 9 сыновей и 3 дочери. Она умерла 4 мая 1658 г. в местности роксбури, суффолк, массачусетц беи колони, бритиш колоняль амирика в возрасте 37 лет и была похоронена в местности роксбури, суффолк, массачусетц беи колони, бритиш колоняль амирика.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Elizabeth Chamberlain nació en 1621, en Hingham, Norfolk, Inglaterra, su papá, Henry Chamberlin Sr, tenía 26 años y su mamá, Jane Grace Freeman, tenía 26 años. Se casó con William Davis aproximadamente 1639, en Roxbury, Suffolk, Massachusetts Bay Colony, British Colonial America. Fueron padres de por lo menos 9 hijos y 3 hijas. Murió el 4 de mayo de 1658, en Roxbury, Suffolk, Massachusetts Bay Colony, British Colonial America, a la edad de 37 años, y fue sepultada en Roxbury, Suffolk, Massachusetts Bay Colony, British Colonial America.
